label: Czech Republic
description: Politics, power brokers and the new face of Central Europe.

items.title: Prague’s Power Brokers And The New Politics Of Central Europe
items.dek: How the Czech Republic is navigating populism, prosperity and pressure from both East and West.
items.body: How the Czech Republic is navigating populism, prosperity and pressure from both East and West. Prague is redefining its role in European politics while balancing domestic reform with EU and NATO commitments.
items.imageAlt: Prague political meeting, two men shaking hands outdoors.

items.title: Czech Government Faces Backlash Over Pension and Tax Reforms
items.dek: Coalition partners clash as public support slips and opposition gains ground.

items.title: EU Presidency Looms as Czechs Weigh Stance on Ukraine and Energy
items.dek: Prague seeks to balance Atlantic solidarity with domestic energy and cost-of-living concerns.
items.imageAlt: EU presidency and Czech stance on Ukraine and energy.

items.title: Czech Opposition Rallies Ahead of Key Regional and Senate Votes
items.dek: Local elections are seen as a bellwether for the next general poll.

label: Slovakia
description: Polarized politics, Eurozone stakes and the Russia question.

items.title: Slovakia’s Polarized Politics And The High Stakes For The Eurozone
items.dek: A deeply divided electorate is testing the country’s institutions—and Europe’s unity on Russia.
items.imageAlt: Bratislava old town with castle at sunset.

items.title: Bratislava Recalibrates Foreign Policy Amid Coalition Tensions
items.dek: The government walks a fine line between EU commitments and domestic populist pressure.

items.title: Slovak Judiciary and Media Reforms Draw Brussels Scrutiny
items.dek: EU rule-of-law mechanisms are again in focus as reforms advance.

items.title: Energy and Defence Deals Put Slovakia at Center of EU Debate
items.dek: Nuclear, gas and military spending decisions ripple through the bloc.

label: Hungary
description: Budapest, Brussels and the battle over Hungary’s political future.

items.title: Power, Brussels And The Battle Over Hungary’s Political Future
items.dek: Inside the tug-of-war between Budapest and Brussels that is reshaping Eastern Europe’s balance of power.
items.imageAlt: Hungarian Parliament Building at dusk by the river.

items.title: EU Funds Remain Frozen as Hungary and Commission Spar Over Reforms
items.dek: Rule-of-law conditionality keeps billions in cohesion and recovery cash on hold.

items.title: Orbán’s Opposition Unites Ahead of Critical Elections
items.dek: A broad coalition is trying to turn local and European gains into national power.

items.title: Hungary’s Stance on Ukraine and Russia Splits NATO and EU Allies
items.dek: Budapest’s vetoes and rhetoric continue to test Western unity.

label: Moldova
description: Reformers, oligarchs and the race for the EU dream.

items.title: Moldova’s Quiet Revolution: Reformers, Oligarchs And The EU Dream
items.dek: Chisinau’s pro‑European government races against time to lock in reforms and fend off Russian influence.
items.imageAlt: Cityscape with government buildings and national flag.

items.title: Chisinau Pushes Judiciary and Anti-Corruption Overhaul for EU Path
items.dek: Reform milestones are key to unlocking membership talks and financial support.

items.title: Transnistria and Gagauzia Keep Moldova’s Unity in Question
items.dek: Frozen conflict and autonomy disputes complicate the country’s European orientation.

items.title: Moldova’s Energy and Security Ties With Romania and EU Deepen
items.dek: Gas, electricity and defence cooperation are reducing dependence on Russia.

label: Georgia
description: Street protests, European path and the choice between Moscow and Brussels.

items.title: Georgia’s Street Protests And The Fight To Stay On A European Path
items.dek: As crowds fill Tbilisi’s avenues, the country’s leaders are forced to choose between Moscow and Brussels.
items.imageAlt: Crowd of people protesting in a city street.

items.title: Tbilisi’s Foreign Agents Law Draws Condemnation From EU and US
items.dek: The controversial bill threatens to derail Georgia’s candidate status and Western support.

items.title: Georgia’s Ruling Party and Opposition Clash Over EU Candidacy
items.dek: Brussels has made clear that backsliding on reforms will have consequences.

items.title: Abkhazia and South Ossetia Cast Shadow Over Georgia’s Sovereignty
items.dek: Russian-backed regions remain a flashpoint as Tbilisi seeks NATO and EU integration.

label: Armenia
description: Strategic crossroads, security and domestic unrest after the conflict.

items.title: After The Conflict: Armenia’s Leaders Face A Strategic Crossroads
items.dek: With security guarantees in flux, Yerevan looks for new partners while managing domestic unrest.
items.imageAlt: Government building and square in Yerevan, Armenia.

items.title: Yerevan Deepens Ties With West as Russia’s Influence Wanes
items.dek: Military and diplomatic shifts signal a historic reorientation.

items.title: Armenia’s Opposition Demands Accountability After Territorial Losses
items.dek: Street protests and resignations keep the government under pressure.

items.title: Border Delimitation and Peace Talks With Azerbaijan Remain Fragile
items.dek: Negotiations continue as both sides weigh ceasefire and long-term settlement.
